title: Text Comprehension
description: Answer questions about the article "The Benefits of Regular Exercise."

questions.question: According to the article, what is the most important thing about exercise?
questions.explanation: The article says: "consistency — exercising regularly is more important than exercising intensely."

questions.question: How long does the article say a walk should be to make a difference?
questions.explanation: "Even a 30-minute walk can make a significant difference."

questions.question: What social benefit of exercise does the article mention?
questions.explanation: "Joining a sports team or a fitness class is a great way to meet new people."

questions.question: The article says that "highlights" means to ___ something. (emphasize/ignore)
questions.explanation: Highlights = emphasizes, draws attention to (resalta, enfatiza).

questions.question: What do experts suggest for people who don't have time?
options: Make small changes like taking stairs
questions.explanation: "Even small changes — like taking the stairs instead of the elevator — can address this issue."

questions.question: What can we INFER about the author's opinion?
options: The author strongly supports regular exercise
The author thinks exercise is overrated
questions.explanation: The tone of the article is clearly positive towards regular exercise.

questions.question: In the text, "address" means to ___ a problem. (deal with/create)
questions.explanation: Address (in this context) = address, address a problem.

questions.question: What results does the article mention from regular exercise?
options: Better sleep, more energy, improved mood
questions.explanation: "People often report better sleep, more energy, and improved mood."

questions.question: The word "issue" in the text most likely means...
questions.explanation: Issue (in context) = problem/challenge that people face.

questions.question: The article uses the connector "___" to add extra information about social benefits.
questions.explanation: "Moreover, exercise has social benefits." —Moreover = in addition (formal addition).

title: Summarize the Text
description: Construct sentences that summarize the main ideas of the article.

sentences.prompt: Summarize the main topic of the article
sentences.correct: The article discusses the benefits of regular exercise

sentences.prompt: What is more important than intensity?
sentences.correct: Consistency is more important than intensity

sentences.prompt: What social benefit does exercise offer?
sentences.correct: Exercise can help you meet new people
Experts suggest to make small changes
Regular exercise leads to better sleep

title: Vocabulary in Context
description: Learn words from the article with their contextual meaning.

cards.front: "The research HIGHLIGHTS the importance of sleep."
cards.back: Highlights = emphasizes, draws attention to (Resalta, enfatiza). Not "the bright parts of hair"!
cards.example: The report highlights several key findings.

cards.back: Consistency = doing something regularly without stopping (Constancia, regularidad).
cards.example: Consistency is the key to success.

cards.back: Address = to deal with, to discuss (Abordar, atender). Issue = problem, topic (Problema, tema).
cards.example: We need to address this issue immediately.
There was a significant improvement in her grades.

cards.back: Despite = in spite of, even though (Despite). Followed by noun or -ing.
cards.example: Despite the rain, we had a wonderful time.

cards.back: Struggle = to try hard but with difficulty.

cards.example: Working with friends helps you stay motivated.

cards.back: Reduce = to make smaller or less (Reducir, disminuir).
Current = present, existing now (Actual, presente). NOT "corriente" (electricity).
cards.example: What is the current status of the project?

cards.back: Routine = a regular series of actions (Rutina). Collocations: daily routine, follow a routine.
cards.example: I follow the same routine every morning.

title: Word → Definition in Context
description: Match each word with its meaning according to the article.
